lessons.title: Deliver Checkmate in 1
lessons.description: Learn to recognize and deliver checkmate with the Queen.

steps.instruction: The Black King is trapped on the edge. Move your Queen to deliver checkmate!

feedback.correct: Excellent! Checkmate! The King has no escape squares.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Look for a Queen move that attacks the King with no escape.

hints: The King is on the edge of the board
Move your Queen to the 8th rank

lessons.title: Queen + King Checkmate
lessons.description: Practice the basic Queen and King checkmate pattern.

steps.instruction: The Black King is near the edge. Give check with the Queen! Play Qh7+

feedback.correct: Check! The Queen controls the 7th rank, forcing the King to the corner.
feedback.incorrect: Try Qh7+ - the Queen gives check along the 7th rank.

steps.instruction: The King retreated to a8. Now bring your King closer with Kb6!

feedback.correct: Your King controls all the escape squares. Black has only one move!
feedback.incorrect: Play Kb6 to cut off all the King's escape routes.

steps.instruction: The King is cornered! Deliver checkmate with Qh8#!

feedback.correct: CHECKMATE! The Queen covers the entire 8th rank while your King blocks all escape squares!
feedback.incorrect: Try Qh8# - the Queen delivers mate on the back rank!

lessons.successMessage: Brilliant! The key to K+Q mate: use your Queen to push the King to the edge, then bring your King to support the final checkmate!
hints: Give check to push the King to the corner
Your King must help by controlling escape squares
Deliver the final blow on the back rank

lessons.title: Castle Kingside
lessons.description: Practice castling to protect your King.

steps.instruction: Castle kingside to get your King to safety! (Type O-O or move King to g1)

feedback.correct: Perfect! Your King is now safe behind pawns, and the Rook is activated!
feedback.incorrect: Castle kingside by moving your King to g1 (it will automatically move the Rook).

lessons.successMessage: Great job! Castling is one of the most important moves for King safety!
hints: Click on your King and move it 2 squares toward the Rook
Castling kingside means the King goes to g1

lessons.title: Control the Center
lessons.description: Learn why center control matters in the opening.

steps.instruction: Start by controlling the center. Play e4!

feedback.correct: e4 occupies the center and opens lines for your Bishop and Queen!
feedback.incorrect: Try e4 - it's one of the best opening moves, controlling d5 and f5.

steps.instruction: Good! Black responded with e5. Now develop your Knight to f3.

feedback.correct: Excellent! The Knight develops naturally and attacks the e5 pawn.
feedback.incorrect: Try Nf3 - Knights go to f3/c3, developing toward the center.

steps.instruction: Black developed too. Now play Bb5 - the Ruy Lopez!

feedback.correct: The Ruy Lopez! A classic opening that prepares castling.
feedback.incorrect: Try Bb5 - this pins the Knight and prepares castling.

lessons.successMessage: You've played the start of the famous Ruy Lopez opening!
hints: Control the center with pawns first
The Ruy Lopez is 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bb5
